message:
fix(missions): WebSocket state-machine guards, reconnect staleness, streaming debounce (#6374)

Addresses three related races in the Mission / WebSocket state machine:

- #6370 — Cancellation stream overwrite. `cancelMission` now records the
  cancel intent in a synchronous ref BEFORE any state update, and the
  message handler routes any terminal message (result / stream-done /
  error) through `finalizeCancellation` when that intent is set. Prevents
  a `result` arriving in the same tick as a cancel click from overwriting
  the mission to `completed`. `finalizeCancellation` no longer requires
  the mission to already be in `cancelling` — it accepts any non-terminal
  status so the synchronous intent wins the race against React's queued
  `cancelling` transition.

- #6371 — Reconnect amnesia zombie. On WS reconnect with
  `needsReconnect=true`, candidates whose last `updatedAt` is older than
  `MISSION_RECONNECT_MAX_AGE_MS` (30 min) are no longer auto-resumed.
  They transition to `failed` with `needsRestart: true` and a banner
  telling the user to start a new session. Frontend-only fix — no new
  backend endpoint required.

- #6372 — State-clobbering under rapid iteration. `useMissionControl`
  now feeds `extractJSON` through `useDebouncedValue` with a
  `STREAM_JSON_DEBOUNCE_MS` (250 ms) trailing debounce. Large Phase 1
  JSON blocks streaming at ~50 tok/s no longer trigger a parse per
  chunk; the balanced-brace scan + `JSON.parse` only runs after the
  stream pauses.

Closes #6370
Closes #6371
Closes #6372

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
